title: Explain this issue in plain language

parts.text: Explain this issue in plain language

It seems like you've asked me to explain an issue, but you haven't provided the specific issue details. Could you share:

- The **error message** or **warning** from your dashboard
- The **code snippet** or **file** involved
- The **category** (e.g., lint error, security finding, type error, etc.)

Once you paste that context, I'll break down:

1. **What's happening** — the root cause in plain language
2. **Why it matters** — the real-world risk
3. **How to fix it** — the smallest safe change

Hey! 👋

It looks like you haven't shared a specific issue yet. To help you out, paste any of the following from your dashboard:

- An **error message** or **warning**
- A **code snippet** with the problem
- A **screenshot description** or **finding details**

I'll break it down in plain language and suggest a fix. What are you working with?

title: Loads over plain HTTP — browsers will block this as mixed content on an HTTPS site.

seed.title: Loads over plain HTTP — browsers will block this as mixed content on an HTTPS site.
seed.summary:
GET request to http://legacy-tracker.net/collect via image. 2 issues detected.
Suggested direction: Loads over plain HTTP — browsers will block this as mixed content on an HTTPS site. Mixed-content request from a secure page; the pixel will be dropped silently.

parts.text: Explain this issue in plain language

Your website is trying to load a tracking pixel from `http://legacy-tracker.net/collect` using an insecure connection (HTTP), but your website itself is secure (HTTPS).

This is called **mixed content**. Modern web browsers will typically block these insecure requests from secure pages because:

1.  **Security Risk**: Even a small insecure asset can potentially be tampered with or expose user information, undermining the overall security of your HTTPS page.
2.  **User Experience**: Browsers might show security warnings to users, or simply silently block the content.

In this specific case, the analytics pixel will **silently fail to load**. This means your application won't be able to track user activity as intended, leading to missing or inaccurate analytics data.

title: What is XSS in one sentence?

parts.text: What is XSS in one sentence?
XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) is a web security vulnerability that allows an attacker to inject malicious client-side scripts into web pages viewed by other users.
